layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — no clean entries yet, patience is the play.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fearful state with most altcoins stuck in downtrends. Bitcoin is holding relatively stable around $64,700 while altcoins are getting crushed — Bitcoin dominance is high at 56.5%, meaning money is flowing away from smaller coins. Every swing candidate on the watchlist either has terrible chart structure or has already run up too much to safely enter. The smartest move right now is to wait for a better setup rather than force a trade into weakness.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch for Ethereum to pull back toward $1,800 support. If it bounces there with increasing volume, that could be a safe entry. Don't chase anything that's already moved 10-20% in the last week.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a compelling risk/reward right now. BONK is in freefall with a -2.0 chart bias — catching that knife is reckless. LDO has the best chart (+0.85 bias, Three White Soldiers) but RSI 59 and +18% in 7 days means it's extended with poor R/R (0.38x). ONDO similarly extended. ETH is the most interesting potential setup: RSI 39 approaching oversold (our highest-conviction signal type at 80% win rate), 5-day momentum turning positive at +17.2%, and clear support at $1,800. However, it's still below all major MAs with a -0.7 chart bias, so I want to see it hold $1,800 support before committing capital. The bearish engulfing on AAVE and high BTC dominance argue against aggressive alt entries right now.
risk_factors: BTC weekend trading with geopolitical oil supply risk (20% of world's oil in balance per CryptoSlate) could trigger a sharp selloff dragging all alts lower
BTC dominance at 56.5% and rising means capital continues rotating away from altcoins — any alt position faces structural headwinds
Fear & Greed at 28 with a flat trend suggests sentiment hasn't bottomed yet — capitulation selling could still intensify
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce with volume confirms entry, a break below invalidates the setup
BTC reaction to $65,000 resistance and whether call spread positioning toward $72K triggers momentum buying
FTX $900M creditor distribution timeline — watch for fresh capital entering the market over coming days
market_context: Crypto market sits in fear territory (28/100) with BTC dominance elevated at 56.5%, creating a hostile environment for altcoin swing trades. BTC is range-bound near $64,700 with options positioning bullish toward month-end, but weekend geopolitical risks add uncertainty.
context_banner: Market fear and high BTC dominance create poor conditions for alt entries — discipline demands patience until support levels are tested and confirmed.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — historically our most reliable signal with 80% win rate, but not yet at the 30 threshold for maximum conviction.
Fear & Greed index at 28 (Fear) with flat trend suggests sentiment hasn't found a floor yet — premature to be aggressive with 62% historical reliability.
FTX distributing $900M to creditors in fifth payment round could inject fresh buying pressure into the market over coming weeks — a potential delayed catalyst.
Weekend geopolitical risk with 20% of world's oil supply in balance creates a binary event risk that argues against opening new positions before Monday.
Every candidate except LDO shows negative chart bias scores; LDO's bullish Three White Soldiers pattern is offset by extended price action (+18% in 7d) and poor 0.38x risk/reward ratio.
